# Documentation Template

Template and writing rules for `src/<id>/README.md` and the optional
`src/<id>/NOTES.md`.

## `src/<id>/README.md` — Required

The README is the canonical documentation for a feature. It follows the format
generated by `devcontainers/action` and is regenerated on each publish. Create
it manually when scaffolding a new feature; the action will keep it up to date.

### Template

````markdown
# <Feature Name> (<id>)

<One sentence: what the feature installs or configures, and why it is useful.>

## Example Usage

```json
"features": {
    "ghcr.io/savvy-web/<id>:0": {}
}
```

## Options

| Options Id | Description | Type | Default Value |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| <optionKey> | <description> | <type> | <default> |

---

_Note: This file was auto-generated from the [devcontainer-feature.json](https://github.com/savvy-web/devcontainers/blob/main/src/<id>/devcontainer-feature.json). Add additional notes to a `NOTES.md`._
````

### Rules

- **Heading** — `# <name> (<id>)`: feature name followed by id in parentheses,
  matching what `devcontainers/action` generates
- **Usage version** — use only the major number (e.g. `0` from `0.1.0`)
- **`## Options` table** — include when the feature has options; omit entirely
  if there are none
- **`## Customizations` section** — include when `customizations.vscode.extensions`
  is set, listing VS Code extension IDs
- **Footer** — always include the auto-gen note and `---` separator
- **File ends with a single newline** — no trailing blank lines

## `src/<id>/NOTES.md` — Optional

Add extra content that the auto-generated template does not cover. The action
appends `NOTES.md` content after the options table (before the `---` footer).

Use `NOTES.md` for:

- Extended examples with non-default options
- OS compatibility or system requirement notes
- Integration guides (e.g. "pair with kcov for coverage")
- Migration notes from a previous version

### Example: `src/package-manager/NOTES.md`

```markdown
## Auto-Detection

When `packageManager` is `"auto"` (the default), the feature searches the
workspace for `package.json` and resolves the package manager spec in this
order:

1. `devEngines.packageManager.name` + `devEngines.packageManager.version`
2. Top-level `packageManager` field
3. If neither is found, corepack shims are enabled (soft no-op)
```

## Existing READMEs as Style Reference

### `src/biome/README.md`

````markdown
# Biome (global linter) (biome)

Installs Biome globally for all runtimes. Strict version, reproducible, and idempotent.

## Example Usage

```json
"features": {
    "ghcr.io/savvy-web/biome:0": {}
}
```

## Options

| Options Id | Description | Type | Default Value |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| biomeVersion | Biome version (absolute, no semver ranges) | string | 2.4.13 |

---

_Note: This file was auto-generated from the [devcontainer-feature.json](...). Add additional notes to a `NOTES.md`._
````

### `src/rust/README.md` (with Customizations section)

```markdown
# Rust Toolchain (Global) (rust)

Installs the Rust toolchain globally using rustup.

...

## Customizations

### VS Code Extensions

- `rust-lang.rust-analyzer`

---

_Note: ..._
```

## Filename Convention

The README must always be at `src/<id>/README.md`. The `documentationURL` in
`devcontainer-feature.json` must point to this file:

```json
"documentationURL": "https://github.com/savvy-web/devcontainers/blob/main/src/<id>/README.md"
```
